title: cassipourea delphinensis
intro: Cassipourea delphinensis, also known as "Sapin-sapin" or "Ligne d'or" in French, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, particularly in countries such as Cameroon, Gabon, and the Republic of Congo. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. Its health benefits include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as being used as a tonic to boost the immune system. Cassipourea delphinensis can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for various herbal preparations, including teas, tonics, and ointments.
